title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_29 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 29 the four-class Γ(Z_p × Z_25) barrier-threshold template still closes cleanly: the fixed barrier set B = {1,19,23,29} spills into only 17 A-labels, while the interval (29,164] contains exactly 28 = p - 1 primes for C. This is the next named boundary feeder for the counting-based slice.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_29 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification. The campaign-relevant gain is that the fixed barrier-threshold package closes exactly at the named boundary p = 29: the 17 nontrivial multiples of 19, 23, and 29 fit inside A, and the interval (29,164] contains exactly 28 = p - 1 primes for C. This is still feeder evidence rather than a proved family theorem.
candidate_theorem_slice: A barrier-threshold sufficient-condition slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if {1,...,5p+19} contains at least p - 1 primes greater than 29 and at most 20 nontrivial multiples of 19, 23, and 29, then taking B = {1,19,23,29}, A as the barrier-multiple reservoir, C from those primes, and D as the complement yields a prime labeling.
next_action: promote the verified p = 29 boundary witness into the family generalization dossier, then test z31-z25-prime-zero-divisor-graph before any exact-instance Lean step
